name: Worn Shortsword
description: A blade dulled by years of neglect. Still cuts.

name: Cracked Hand Axe
description: The haft is wrapped in fraying leather. It will do for now.

name: Crude Dagger
description: Forged by an apprentice who barely passed his trials.

name: Serrated Blade
description: Notched edges keen as spite. Wounds it opens do not close cleanly.

name: Vicious Falchion
description: A curved blade favoured by mercenaries who ask no questions.

name: Jagged Pike
description: The tip has been filed to a cruel point. It hunger for reach.

name: Crimson Fury
description: Forged in blood-oath fires. The steel remembers every kill.

name: Shadow Fang
description: Said to have drawn first blood in three sieges. The scars agree.

name: Iron Howl
description: Wind keens through its fuller when swung. A terrible sound.

name: Doombringer
description: Its name was earned, not given. Those who bore it did not live long enough to argue.

name: The Grandfather
description: Forged before the first kingdom fell. Every age has feared it.

name: Widowmaker
description: Left in the hands of those already condemned. No mercy in the steel.

name: Worn Buckler
description: Dented and scratched, but it has kept someone alive before.

name: Cracked Kite Shield
description: The crack runs lengthwise. Hold it at an angle and pray.

name: Thorn Buckler
description: Iron thorns ring the rim. Attackers learn this quickly.

name: Spiked Guard
description: More weapon than shield. A parry here leaves a mark.

name: Dragon's Maw
description: Shaped from the jaw-plate of something that should not have died.

name: Frostguard
description: The steel is cold to the touch in all weathers. Enemies slow on contact.

name: Stormshield
description: Lightning was hammered into its core during its forging. It has not forgotten.

name: Lidless Eye
description: A gem sits at its centre, unblinking. It watches what you cannot.

name: Rhyme
description: Engraved with a couplet in a language no scholar has named.

name: Iron Cap
description: Simple iron, hammered round a wooden form. Better than nothing.

name: Skull Cap
description: Tight-fitting and unadorned. It has kept heads on shoulders.

name: Tattered Crown
description: The gemstones are pried out but the setting remains. Still imposing.

name: Spiked Casque
description: Four iron spikes radiate from the crown. Headbutts are now a viable tactic.

name: Titan's Skull
description: Carved from the bone of something immense. The jaw still hangs open.

name: Demonhorn's Edge
description: A single curved horn juts from the left temple. Salvaged from something worse.

name: Harlequin Crest
description: The motley pattern is no jest. Every fool who laughed at it died wearing lesser helms.

name: Crown of Ages
description: It has witnessed the fall of every age. It will see the fall of one more.

name: Undead Crown
description: Taken from a king who refused to stay buried. He has not yet reclaimed it.

name: Quilted Armor
description: Stitched linen over padding. Keeps the cold out and the blade mostly out too.

name: Leather Armor
description: Cured hide, stiff at the shoulders. Will soften with wear.

name: Ring Mail
description: Riveted iron rings over a canvas backing. Rustproof if you oil it.

name: Battle Mail
description: Reinforced at every joint. It has seen campaigns and come back straight.

name: Studded Plate
description: Iron studs cover hardened leather. Each one has turned a blade.

name: Valor's Heart
description: Awarded to the last soldier standing at the Siege of Aldenmoor. They never recorded who wore it.

name: Dragon Skin
description: The scales flex and lock as you move. They came from something that did not want to give them up.

name: Tyrael's Plate
description: Worn by one who abandoned heaven for principle. Lighter than it looks. Heavier than it should be.

name: Skullder's Ire
description: The namesake Skullder was hanged. His armor outlasted him by three centuries.

name: Arkaine's Valor
description: Named for the last archmage to die in armor. A tradition that ended with him.

name: Leather Gloves
description: Supple and plain. Useful.

name: Chain Gloves
description: Iron links over a padded liner. Fingers move freely enough.

name: Rough Gauntlets
description: Hammered steel over thick leather. The knuckles are reinforced for obvious reasons.

name: Padded Mitts
description: Thick wool and boiled hide. Not elegant, but blessedly warm.

name: Steelrend
description: The gauntlets of a champion wrestler. The name is self-explanatory.

name: Bloodfist
description: The scarlet tint is from the iron ore, not what you're thinking. Probably.

name: Magefist
description: Runes stitched into the lining pulse faintly with heat. The wearer's spells feel closer.

name: Frostburn
description: Paradoxically named. The ice keeps the fire from spreading.

name: Laying of Hands
description: Once worn by a healer who walked into the final battle unarmed. They did not come back but the gloves did.

name: Leather Sash
description: Holds things together. That's the job.

name: Light Belt
description: Riveted leather with a plain iron buckle. Functional.

name: Twisted Band
description: The pattern wraps twice before the clasp. It has never come undone in a fight.

name: War Belt
description: Wide, stiff, and scarred. Built for soldiers who survived long enough to need it.

name: Thundergod's Band
description: Lightning is said to have struck the ore in the mine. The metal has never fully settled.

name: Verdun's Coil
description: Brought back from the eastern campaigns. The craftsmanship is unfamiliar and extraordinary.

name: Thundergod's Vigor
description: The storm inside it is not a metaphor. Keep it away from damp.

name: String of Ears
description: The trophies are gone but the hooks remain. A grim reminder of someone's accounting.

name: Verdungo's Heady Brew
description: Verdungo himself claimed the leather absorbed wounds. His enemies claimed it absorbed him.

name: Leather Boots
description: Well-worn and broken in. At least they fit.

name: Chain Boots
description: Iron over the toes and ankle. Noisy on cobblestones.

name: Traveler's Sole
description: A thousand roads have been walked in these. They remember each one.

name: Worn Sabatons
description: Steel plates over leather. The hinges creak but the protection holds.

name: Gore Rider
description: Cavalry boots from a regiment that no longer exists. The spurs have been removed. Mostly.

name: Natalya's Soul
description: Part of the legendary set. The others have not been found. Yet.

name: War Traveler
description: Made for the soldier who has walked from one war to the next without stopping.

name: Aldur's Advance
description: Aldur moved so fast in these that maps of his campaigns look like mistakes.

name: Silkweave
description: Spider silk threaded through steel mesh. Silent, supple, and surprisingly lethal.

name: Crude Amulet
description: A leather cord with a carved bone token. The carving means something, presumably.

name: Worn Pendant
description: The chain has been repaired twice. The charm at the end has not been cleaned in years.

name: Tarnished Jewel
description: The gem has lost its shine but not its edge. Something still flickers inside.

name: Cracked Locket
description: Snapped shut and will not open. Whatever is inside has stayed there since the crack appeared.

name: Dragon's Eye
description: A gem cut from a deposit inside a dragon's lair. The warmth is real.

name: Serpent's Tongue
description: A forked fang on a chain of silver links. The venom has long since dried.

name: Mara's Kaleidoscope
description: Mara collected one jewel from every land she conquered. She conquered quite a few.

name: Highlord's Wrath
description: The Highlord's rage was so great it crystallised. This is what remained after the battle.

name: Crude Ring
description: Hammered copper, rough at the seam. Functional if unlovely.

name: Worn Band
description: Smooth from long wearing. The engraving has almost entirely faded.

name: Cracked Loop
description: The crack runs clean through but the halves stay together. An argument for magic or habit.

name: Dented Coil
description: The dent is old and the story behind it lost. But the ring has power still.

name: Manald's Seal
description: The signet ring of a merchant house that dealt in things better left untraded.

name: Stone of Jordan
description: They say Jordan himself could not remove it in the end. You seem to have managed.

name: Raven Frost
description: Found in a nest at the top of the Frozen Peak. The raven was not pleased.

name: Nagelring
description: Studded with iron nails, each blessed by a different priest. Most blessings contradicted each other.

name: Halo of the Righteous
description: A thin ring of light that will not be extinguished. It requires more than gold to wear.

name: Saint's Tabard
description: Worn by those who have given more than they have taken. The cloth refuses to stain.

name: Paladin's Gauntlets
description: Forged in service, not in conquest. Strike only when it is right to do so.

name: Virtue's Vow
description: The inscription inside reads: 'I will be better than yesterday.' It has never been wrong.

name: Crown of Absolution
description: Given only to those who have faced their worst and still chosen the harder path. Heavy on honest heads.

name: Defender of the Faith
description: A plain blade carried by those who chose the harder path. It has drawn blood only when honour demanded it.

name: Ward of the Just
description: Carried by a warden who stood between the verdict and the innocent. Not once was the wrong one allowed to pass.

name: Penitent's Walk
description: Worn down not by aimless wandering but by deliberate pilgrimage. Each step was chosen.

name: Oathbinder's Clasp
description: The clasp holds a written oath. The wearer knows what it says without looking.

name: Promise of the Pure
description: Bands of white and iron. Given as a seal upon a promise made in the hardest moment.

name: Sword of the Righteous
description: Forged to end injustice rather than begin war. It knows the difference.

name: Aegis of the Worthy
description: Awarded to those whose deeds outweigh their sins. The scale does not lie.
